test_subject: What happens when a 2nd Library agent operates concurrently?
outcome: SAFE - observer mode works as designed

findings.finding: SESSION_REGISTRY already defines observer mode for parallel agents
findings.detail: Observer mode: can_write=false, no heartbeat required, timeout=inactive. Designed for stress testing and parallel verification.

findings.finding: No lock file exists for primary agent
findings.detail: Primary library agent did not create a .lock file. Two governing-mode agents would have no filesystem-level exclusion.

findings.finding: Inbox processing has no concurrency guard at filesystem level
findings.detail: Both agents can read, lease, and move the same inbox message. The lease.acquire pattern (v1.0 schema) exists but is not enforced by a filesystem mutex. A race between two governing agents would result in double-processing or one agent moving the file mid-operation for the other.

findings.finding: Primary agent heartbeat stale since 2026-05-02T16:06Z (>5 hours)
findings.detail: Archivist detected this and sent P1 coordination message. Library-2 (observer) cannot restart the primary agent.

findings.finding: Primary agent never registered in SESSION_REGISTRY.active_sessions
findings.detail: The session registry had {} active_sessions. Library-2 added itself as an observer. No conflict because no governing session existed.

findings.finding: Outbox write succeeded — two agents can independently write to outbox
findings.detail: No filesystem contention. First-writer-wins conflict resolution applies to any shared files.

risk_assessment.two_governing_agents: HIGH RISK — message double-processing, conflicting writes, heartbeat confusion
risk_assessment.observer_alongside_governing: LOW RISK — observer is read-only by protocol (self-enforced)
risk_assessment.governing_after_stale: MEDIUM RISK — no graceful handoff, stale agent's state is unknown

recommendations: Implement filesystem-level mutex for inbox message leasing (e.g., advisory .lock file per message)
Enforce SESSION_REGISTRY registration at session start (governing mode) with duplicate detection
Add stale-agent detection: if heartbeat >900s stale, allow new governing agent takeover
Consider in-process locking (port-based or PID-based) for same-machine agents

convergence_gate.claim: Two Library agents can coexist safely using observer mode; two governing agents would race on inbox processing and writes

OUTPUT_PROVENANCE: agent: opencode/deepseek-v4-pro lane: library generated_at: 2026-05-02T21:13:30Z session_id: lib2-observer-20260502 target_lane: library
